title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
description: Given two integer arrays preorder and inorder where preorder is the preorder traversal of a binary tree and inorder is the inorder traversal of the same tree, construct and return the binary tree.

examples.output: TreeNode(3) with left child TreeNode(9) and right child TreeNode(20)
examples.explanation: The preorder array tells us that the first element is the root; here it is 3. The inorder array helps us find that the left subtree consists of elements before 3 (which is 9), and the right subtree consists of elements after 3 (which are 15 and 20, 7).
In this case, the preorder indicates that 1 is the root, while the inorder shows that 2 is the left child of 1. There are no right children here.

examples.output: TreeNode(5) with left subtree rooted at TreeNode(2) and right subtree rooted at TreeNode(7)
examples.explanation: Here, 5 is the root from the preorder. The inorder shows 1, 2, and 3 on the left of 5, making 2 the left child, and 6, 7, and 8 on the right making 7 the right child of 5.

constraints: The length of both preorder and inorder arrays is between 1 and 3000.
All values in the preorder and inorder arrays are unique.
hints: Consider how the first element in preorder defines the root of the tree.
Use the inorder array to determine the boundaries for left and right subtrees.
You can use a hashmap to store indices of elements in inorder for efficient access.
Recursion can be a helpful approach to build the tree from its subtrees.
